==Biography==
Kang "'''Cpt Jack'''" Hyung-woo started playing Galaga and Doom at the age of 6 on his family's computer. When he was 8, he was given the game StarCraft and beat the campaign in a single day. Kang went on to play several other titles before finding League of Legends in high school, and his first champion was {{ci|Ashe}}. Kang's first competitive League of Legends experience was a small tournament that he signed up to play in, where he met [[Helios (Shin Dong-jin)|Helios]], who was on another team. A later tournament found him playing against [[MiG Blaze]] in the finals, and he was then recruited to the team.

===Season 2===
Cpt Jack joined the original MiG Blaze roster near the end of 2011 alongside Helios, [[Lustboy]], [[Ambition]], and [[Reapered]]. The team played in their first tournament
during [[Azubu The Champions Spring 2012]] where they upset their sister team [[MiG Frost]] 3-0 in the finals. Blaze and Frost were then sponsored by Azubu, becoming [[Azubu Blaze]] and [[Azubu Frost]]. A few months later, they flew to America to participate in [[MLG Summer Arena]] where they only dropped one game during the whole tournament and beat [[Team SoloMid]] 2-0 in the finals. Blaze then came back to Korea for [[Azubu The Champions Summer 2012]] but placed fourth after losing 3-2 to Frost in the semifinals and then 2-0 to [[NaJin Sword]] in the third-place match. Blaze earned enough circuit points to qualify for the [[Season 2/Regional Finals - Seoul|Season 2 Korea Regional Finals]] but they lost 3-2 to Sword in the finals. Blaze traveled to America to compete in the [[2012 MLG Pro Circuit/Fall/Championship|2012 MLG Fall Championship]] with [[Shy]] as their sub for top lane. They took first in the tournament, beating Sword in two sets, 2-1 and 2-0. Blaze then went to [[IPL 5]] and placed 7th-8th after losing 2-1 to [[Moscow 5]] in the loser's bracket.

===Season 3===
Blaze's first tournament of the new year was [[IEM Season VII - Global Challenge Katowice|IEM Katowice]] and they made it to the finals but lost 2-0 to [[Gambit Gaming]]. Their time at [[OLYMPUS Champions Winter 2012-2013]] ended just a few days later when they lost two straight 3-2 sets to Frost and then lost 3-0 to [[KT Rolster B]] in the third-place match to ultimately end fourth. Shortly after, Blaze and Frost left the organization and were picked up by CJ Entus. The teams became [[CJ Entus Blaze]] and [[CJ Entus Frost]]. Next, Blaze went to the [[IEM Season VII - World Championship]] where they beat Frost 3-1 to take the tournament. They came back to Korea for [[OLYMPUS Champions Spring 2013]] and made it all the way to the finals. They went into the match against [[MVP Ozone]] as the favorites but they shockingly lost 3-0 in what became one of the biggest upsets in League of Legends' history. They came into [[HOT6iX Champions Summer 2013]] with high hopes but dropped out in the quarterfinals 3-2 against the KT Rolster Bullets. Blaze acquired enough circuit points to qualify for the [[Season 3 Korea Regional Finals]] and lost in the first round 3-0 to the Bullets. Blaze qualified for the [[2013 World Cyber Games/Main Tournament|World Cyber Games 2013]] and beat [[OMG]] 2-0 in the finals.

===2014 Season===
At the first OGN of the new season, [[PANDORA.TV Champions Winter 2013-2014]], Frost qualified for the playoffs but lost 3-0 to [[Samsung Ozone]]. Cpt Jack left the team after this tournament to join the [[Jin Air Green Wings Stealths]]. Cpt Jack played his first tournament with the team at [[HOT6iX Champions Summer 2014]] where they made it to the quarterfinals but lost 3-0 to [[Samsung Blue]].

===2015 Season===
Changes to the OGN rules forced the Stealths and their sister team, the [[Jin Air Green Wings Falcons]], to merge to create the [[Jin Air Green Wings]]. Cpt Jack was set to share starting time with Falcons' AD, [[Pilot (Na Woo-hyung)|Pilot]]. Jin Air performed extremely well for most of [[SBENU Champions Spring 2015]] and took the fourth seed going into the playoffs. However, they faltered towards the end of the season and ended up losing 3-0 to [[CJ Entus]] in the quarterfinals, taking fourth for the season. Jin Air then attended [[SBENU Champions Summer 2015]] but placed 6th overall, gaining 10 circuit points and qualifying for the next season of LCK but barely missing playoffs. Jin Air was seeded into the first round of the [[2015 Season Korea Regional Finals]] and made it all the way to the finals but they lost 3-1 to [[KT Rolster]]; however, Cpt Jack didn't play in any of the games.

===2016 Season===
Jin Air played in the 2015 LoL KeSPA Cup where they lost 2-0 in the quarterfinals to CJ Entus. Cpt Jack didn't play in these games. Cpt Jack left Jin Air in November 2015, and in December he joined [[Incredible Miracle]].<ref>[http://www.facebook.com/IMteam/posts/970197939729690 Incredible Miracle's Facebook post] ''facebook.com''</ref> Incredible Miracle rebranded to [[Longzhu]] on January 3, 2016.

During the [[LCK/2016 Season/Spring Season|2016 LCK Spring Season]], Longzhu played with a 10-man roster, liberally using substitute players. Cpt Jack shared play time with fellow ADC [[Fury (Lee Jin-yong)|Fury]]. Longzhu finished the spring split in seventh place with an 8-10 set record, missing out on a spot in the playoffs. After the spring split in May, Cpt Jack left Longzhu to join SPOTV as one of their Korean language casters for the [[LCK/2016 Season/Summer Season|2016 LCK Summer Season]].<ref>[http://www.fomos.kr/esports/news_view?entry_id=26746 '캡틴잭' 강형우, 해설자로 '인생 2막' 도전 (Korean)] ''fomos.kr''</ref>

== Trivia ==
* Favorite AD carries are {{ci|Ashe}} and {{ci|Vayne}}.<ref>[http://www.thisisgame.com/board/view.php?id=1124185&category=13735 <nowiki>[와드]</nowiki>#4. 우리의 저력을 보여줄 차례다, MiG Blaze (Korean)] ''thisisgame.com''</ref>
* Favorite player is [[WeiXiao]].
* Known for perfect uses of summoner spell [[Cleanse]] and {{ci|Sivir}}'s Spell Shield.
* Awarded Best AD Carry at 2012 Korean e-Sports Awards. <ref>[http://www.inven.co.kr/webzine/news/?site=lol&news=80305 Result of 2012 Korean e-Sports Awards]''inven.com.kr''</ref>
* Said he felt happier when his new team [[Stealths|Jin Air Stealths]] beat his old team [[CJ Entus Blaze]] to advance to the quarterfinals of [[HOT6iX Champions Summer 2014]] than when he won the OGN Championship in 2012.
* Decided on the name "Cpt Jack" because he liked a character that was a sparrow in another game.
* Wants to become a game developer when he retires from competitive gaming.
* Was the substitute for the LCK All-Stars at [[All-Star Los Angeles 2015]] and was pick/ban coach during the finals against the EU LCS All-Stars, coached by [[Niels]].
* Has a "special" relationship with Faker in solo queue because Faker always plays Alistar support and feeds whenever he is playing with Cpt Jack. Fans jokingly comment "Uf! A tearful sacrifice!" whenever Cpt Jack is mentioned.
